title: Rapunzel
content:
Long ago, a husband and wife lived beside a walled garden belonging to a powerful witch. They longed for a child, and at last their wish came true. But during her wait, the woman looked over the wall and saw a beautiful green herb called rapunzel growing there. She longed for it so intensely that she fell ill.

Her husband crept into the witch's garden at night and gathered some of the herb. His wife felt much better, but her craving grew stronger. He crept in again, and this time the witch was waiting. "You will give me your child in payment," she hissed. The man, terrified, agreed.

When the baby was born, the witch took her and named her Rapunzel, after the herb. She locked the girl in a tall stone tower deep in the forest. The tower had no door and no stairs. The only opening was a single window at the very top.

Rapunzel grew up in her tower, singing to fill the silence. She had extraordinarily long golden hair, so long it could hang from her window all the way to the ground. When the witch visited, she called: "Rapunzel, Rapunzel, let down your golden hair!" Rapunzel would lower her braids, and the witch would climb up.

One afternoon, a prince riding through the forest heard a voice of heartbreaking beauty floating from the tower. He found no door. He hid in the trees and watched until the witch arrived. He memorized the magic words.

The next evening, the prince called out the words himself. Rapunzel was startled to see a stranger. But the prince was kind and gentle, and soon they were talking for hours. He returned each night, and before long they had fallen deeply in love. They made a plan to escape together.

But the witch discovered the secret. In her fury she cut off Rapunzel's hair and banished her to a faraway desert wasteland. That evening, she held the cut braids out the window. When the prince climbed up, the witch was waiting. She pushed him from the tower into thorn bushes below, and the thorns blinded him.

The prince wandered for many months, blind and heartbroken. He had nearly given up hope entirely when he heard singing, a voice he recognized above all others.

He stumbled forward and found Rapunzel in the wasteland. She wept with joy when she saw him. Her tears fell upon his eyes, and when they touched him, his sight returned. He could see again! They walked out of the wilderness together and found a kingdom that welcomed them gladly. They were never parted again for the rest of their happy lives.

author: Brothers Grimm
